description: An IIR filter output grows without bound or rings forever because a pole was placed on or outside the unit circle.
body: Failure mode: feedback coefficients put a pole at or beyond unit radius, so the recursive output diverges or sustains oscillation. Detection signals: output amplitude climbing each sample, a sustained tone after the input stops, and a computed pole radius at or above one. Do not deploy the filter while any pole sits outside the unit circle. Safer fallback: redesign with poles strictly inside the unit circle or factor into stable second-order sections and re-verify pole radii.
